name: Sierra de Majalinos Route
name_original: Sierra Majalinos — circular Norte Teruel (BTT02) desde Ejulve
description: Tackle the difficult 38.1-mile Sierra de Majalinos Route, a mountain biking challenge with 4311 feet of climbing and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ejulve are set within the rugged landscapes of Teruel province in Aragon, Spain. The region features diverse terrain, including thick forests, high-mountain areas, and valleys carved by rivers like the Guadalope. Mountain bikers can expect significant elevation changes and varied natural features, making it suitable for different disciplines. Best mountain bike trails around Ejulve The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Majalinos Route, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging terrain with notable elevation gains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de los Caballos - Santolea Trail, a moderate 24.2 miles (38.9 km) path. This trail winds through varied landscapes, including forest sections and open areas. Local mountain bikers also love the El Raspador, Puente del Vado y Órganos de Montoro — circular por el Valle de Guadalope (BTT07), a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through the Guadalope Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Ejulve is defined by rugged landscapes, thick forests, and the great furrow opened by the Guadalope River. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Ejulve are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 3 reviews. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ejulve's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect around Ejulve?
A: The Ejulve region, nestled in Teruel province, offers a diverse and rugged landscape ideal for mountain biking. You'll encounter thick forests, challenging high-mountain areas, and valleys carved by rivers like the Guadalope. Expect significant elevation changes and varied natural features, suitable for XC, marathon, trail, and enduro disciplines.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ejulve?
A: While many trails in Ejulve feature moderate to difficult terrain with significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy route available. The overall network includes options for various ability levels, but most routes lean towards moderate or difficult, making it more suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Ejulve?
A: Yes, Ejulve offers several excellent circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995602294/sierra-de-garrocha-circular-desde-el-portal-de-marzo-de-cuevas-de-canart" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Garrocha — circular desde el Portal de Marzo de Cuevas de Cañart&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.5 km loop. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996660271/el-raspador-puente-del-vado-y-organos-de-montoro-circular-por-el-valle-de-guadalope-btt07" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Raspador, Puente del Vado y Órganos de Montoro — circular por el Valle de Guadalope (BTT07)&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.6 km moderate trail through the Guadalope Valley.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover along the mountain bike trails?
A: Ejulve's trails are rich with natural beauty. You can ride through lush forests and along the great furrow opened by the Guadalope River. A notable highlight is the Mirador de los Órganos, offering magnificent views over Las Hoces del Guadalope, Montoro de Mezquita, and the straits of Boca del Infierno. You might also encounter a sickle with an active cascade during heavy rains, and spot indigenous crayfish, cliff-nesting birds of prey, and mountain goats.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to see while mountain biking near Ejulve?
A: Yes, the region boasts impressive natural monuments. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt; and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features add to the scenic appeal of the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ejulve?
A: The diverse terrain of Ejulve makes it suitable for mountain biking across various seasons. However, given the rugged landscape and potential for active cascades during heavy rains, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter conditions might present challenges in higher elevations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ejulve?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ejulve are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Riders often praise the rugged landscapes, the thick forests, and the challenging yet rewarding routes that offer significant elevation changes and diverse natural features.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible from the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Ejulve offers unique cultural and historical attractions. The trails often weave through areas with traditional farmhouses, known as 'masadas,' representing a nearly lost way of life. You can also explore the old marked footpaths that connect these sites, providing insight into the region's heritage.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails in the Ejulve area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Ejulve offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999030439/sierra-de-majalinos-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Majalinos Route&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 61.3 km trail with over 1300 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995603265/hoces-del-guadalope-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Guadalope Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 68.1 km with nearly 1800 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any caves or geological formations to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the broader Teruel province, where Ejulve is located, is known for its natural geological formations. While not directly on every trail, attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grutas de Cristal&lt;/a&gt; (Crystal Caves) are within reasonable distance and offer a fascinating geological experience.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Ejulve region?
A: There are 15 mountain bike trails available around Ejulve, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for different preferences and experience levels.
tips: We are in the beautiful town of Ejulve, located in the Andorra-Sierra de Arcos region. We, adventurers, highlight this town for being the starting point of The Silent Route tourist route that covers several of the most outstanding places in the region by bicycle. Throughout the municipality of Ejulve is the Órganos de Montoro Natural Monument, the Cueva del Recuenco, the Sierra de Majalinos or the Masías de Ejulve, which also have their own BTT route.
Right next to the road we will find one of the beautiful Ejulve farmhouses belonging to route 1 of the NorteTeruel BTT Center.
We deviate from the path to ascend to this point located at 1601 meters above sea level in the Sierra de Majalinos with beautiful panoramic views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Majalinos Route is a challenging ride that generally takes around 6 hours and 10 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, technical skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Majalinos Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills due to significant elevation changes, technical sections, and potentially unpaved, difficult-to-ride segments. Some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, featuring a mix of dirt paths, technical descents, and unpaved sections. Riders should be prepared for loose soil, potential rock gardens, and steep inclines. The route includes mountain passes with average slopes of 5% over several kilometers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Majalinos Route?
faqs.answer: A good starting point for this circular route is the village of Ejulve Village. You should be able to find parking within or near the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Majalinos Route is part of the 'Centro BTT Norte Teruel' network. While specific permits for individual rides are generally not required, it's always a good idea to check the official Centro BTT Norte Teruel website for any current regulations, fees, or specific trail conditions before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sierra de Majalinos Route?
faqs.answer: For the best experience, consider riding during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Clear winter mornings can offer spectacular panoramic views, including the snow-covered Pyrenees from Pico Majalinos, but be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow on higher elevations. Avoid the peak summer months due to high temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Majalinos Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region. However, given the route's 'difficult' rating and technical terrain, ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, challenging ride. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, and carry enough water for both of you, as water sources may be limited.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through Ejulve Village and near Masía Cabezano. A major highlight is the Majalinos Summit Observation Tower, located on Pico Majalinos (1,601 m), which provides extensive panoramic views of the surrounding area, including distant mountain ranges on clear days.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known mountain biking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route extensively covers sections of the 'Sierra de Majalinos' trail (97% of the route) and also includes portions of 'Masías de Ejulve' (55%). Smaller segments also overlap with 'Subida a Majalinos' (16%) and 'El Raspador' (12%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The village of Ejulve Village, which serves as a common starting point, offers basic amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for mountain bikers. It's advisable to check availability and opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patches, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is highly recommended for navigation, as some sections may be remote.
